text.title: Observability Architecture Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Observability Architecture` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every service MUST emit all three pillars (metrics + logs + traces) with shared correlation IDs (trace_id, span_id). Pillar-only setups = INCOMPLETE.
SLIs (latency, error rate, saturation, freshness) are declared BEFORE SLOs. SLO without SLI = REJECT.
SLO alerts use multi-burn-rate (e.g. 2% in 1h + 5% in 6h) — single-threshold alerts cause page-fatigue + slow detection.
rule.rationale: Single-rate = high false-positive OR slow detection; multi-rate balances.

rule.statement: Production trace sampling MUST be tail-based for trace volume > 1k spans/s — head sampling drops the interesting traces.
Log + trace retention is tiered by criticality (hot 7d / warm 30d / cold 1y). Uniform long retention = budget runaway.
rule.rationale: Uniform retention is the leading observability-cost driver.

rule.statement: Logs MUST be structured (JSON) with required fields (timestamp, severity, service, trace_id, request_id). Free-form text = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Free-form logs are not queryable; correlation breaks.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
